tasks.title: Fast response with similars
tasks.problem: until the server receives a response from the instance, the client shows an empty page; some instances respond slowly.
tasks.solution_option: first show similar videos (fast response from local DB), then load the current video metadata when it arrives.
concrete_steps: When opening VideoPage the client starts two independent processes in parallel: a fast request for similars and a request for metadata of the current video.
The server responds to the similars request immediately from local cache/DB, without waiting for live instance responses, so the UI is not empty.
Metadata updates are done via a separate request to the instance (server-side or client-side), then saved to the DB and the UI updates.

tasks.title: Similars on scroll
tasks.problem: only 8 videos are shown in similar videos; there is a need to see more.
tasks.solution_option: remove the separate "similar videos" page and load similars directly on the video page, like on the home page: the server returns N similars and the client renders them progressively as you scroll.
concrete_steps: The server for video-page returns a full batch of similars at once (for example, BATCH_SIZE = 48) in a single response so the client does not make frequent requests.
The client keeps the batch in memory and shows it in chunks while scrolling, adding new cards as the user nears the bottom.

tasks.title: Video page metadata completeness: show tags/category + refresh mutable fields
tasks.problem: video page does not show all useful metadata (especially tags and category), and metadata refresh on video request currently focuses mostly on dynamic stats (views/likes) while other fields may also change.
tasks.solution_option: render tags and category on the video page, and extend per-request metadata refresh to update mutable video fields from the source instance.
concrete_steps: UI changes (video page):
Add visible blocks for:
category (or category label),
tags (list/chips, with empty-state if none).
Keep layout compact and consistent with existing metadata area.
API/server refresh on video request:
Keep dynamic stats refresh (views, likes) as-is.
Also refresh mutable metadata fields from instance response:
title,
description,
tags,
category,
optional: language, nsfw, duration, thumbnail_url if returned.
Update local DB in one write path with clear mapping rules.
Data model consistency:
Ensure tags/category are stored in DB schema used by /api/video.
If category is numeric in source API, map to display label consistently.
Robustness:
Current behavior (already implemented in server/api/handlers/video.py):
Instance fetch uses urlopen(..., timeout=8) and catches HTTPError, URLError, TimeoutError.
On such errors (or non-200), instance JSON returns None, and dynamic payload becomes empty/partial.
Response to client is built with DB fallback field-by-field (title/description/views/likes/dislikes/tags/category/nsfw), so UI still gets data from local DB.
DB update (UPDATE videos/channels) runs only when dynamic payload is non-empty; on instance failure no overwrite is performed.
Instance error reset (instances.last_error = NULL) also happens only on successful dynamic refresh path.
Keep this fallback logic and document it explicitly in implementation notes/tests.
Validation / tests:
Manual test: open video page, verify tags/category render.
Integration test: source metadata change is reflected after next /api/video request.
DB persistence test on a test database:
use a test DB that mirrors production schema (same tables/columns/indexes for videos/channels/instances);
run /api/video requests against known fixtures (success and instance-fail cases);
assert saved values in DB after request (title/description/views/likes/dislikes/tags_json/category/nsfw/last_checked_at);
assert no overwrite happens on failed instance fetch path.
Regression test: stats refresh still works and does not wipe existing tags/category on partial responses.

tasks.title: Comments under the video
tasks.problem: comments are not displayed under the video.
tasks.solution_option: the server or client requests the instance to get comments and render them under the video on the page.
concrete_steps: Add comment fetching via instance API (or a server proxy) and render them under the video description block.
UX: show a placeholder/loader, support pagination and limits (in batches) to avoid overloading the page.
Remove the comment input field (view-only).
First verify on a specific video which request is needed to fetch comments; use the response structure for client rendering.

tasks.title: Tailwind CSS (optional)
tasks.problem: styles are fragmented and hard to maintain.
tasks.solution_option: evaluate Tailwind and adopt if needed.
concrete_steps: Start with new blocks, gradually replace repeating styles.

tasks.title: Switchable feed modes: recommendations / hot / recent / random / popular
tasks.problem: right now there are only recommendations and random; there are no quick modes like “only recent / only hot / only popular.”
tasks.solution_option: add feed modes on the home page and a mode switch.
concrete_steps: UI: mode switcher (recommendations, hot, recent, random, popular).
API: mode parameter (or separate endpoints) to fetch the corresponding feed.
Hot: separate formula (e.g., likes/views with age decay).
Recent: only fresh items (by published_at).
Popular: top by likes/views.
Random: random feed.

tasks.title: About page outbound links: proper click tracking
tasks.problem: clicks on external links from about.html are currently visible only indirectly in nginx logs and are noisy/inaccurate because bots and malformed requests are mixed in.
tasks.solution_option: add explicit client event tracking for outbound link clicks on the About page and store events in API DB.
concrete_steps: Client instrumentation:
Add data-track-id for each outbound link in client/frontend/about.html (e.g., about_patreon, about_github, about_youtube).
Add one delegated click handler for a[data-track-id].
Send event with navigator.sendBeacon() to /api/analytics/outbound-click (fallback to fetch(..., { keepalive: true }) if needed).
Event payload: track_id, href, page_path, timestamp.
API endpoint:
Add POST /api/analytics/outbound-click.
Validate payload (track_id, href, allowlist of known IDs/hosts).
Write event row to SQLite table (append-only).
DB schema:
New table outbound_click_events with fields:
id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
track_id TEXT NOT NULL
href TEXT NOT NULL
page_path TEXT NOT NULL
created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
optional diagnostics: ip_hash, user_agent, referer
Add index on (track_id, created_at).
Reporting:
Add simple aggregated query/endpoint for counts by track_id (daily and total).
Keep raw events for audit/debug.
Abuse/noise control:
Rate-limit endpoint per IP.
Reject unknown track_id values.
Keep privacy-safe storage (no raw IP if not needed; hash or omit).
Validation / tests:
Client test: click dispatch sends correct payload.
API test: valid event is stored, invalid event is rejected.
E2E smoke test: clicking About links increases counter in DB.

tasks.title: Remove a single like (UI + API)
tasks.problem: currently you can only reset all likes, but cannot remove a single one.
tasks.solution_option: let the user remove a like from the list (in the My likes modal) and/or via dislike on the video page.
concrete_steps: UI: remove button on like card in the modal.
Video page: dislike as “remove like” (if the video was liked).
Client: remove entry from localStorage (uuid/host).
Server: a dedicated endpoint (or extend /user-profile/reset) to delete a single like from the users DB, if a DB is used.

tasks.title: Collapsible video description
tasks.problem: long video descriptions take up too much space.
tasks.solution_option: implement collapse/expand for the description.
concrete_steps: Limit description height and show a “Show more/Collapse” button.
On click, toggle expanded/collapsed state and keep it in the UI.

tasks.title: Video search page
tasks.problem: there is no search page and no server-side logic for video search.
tasks.solution_option: API search (FTS/LIKE) and a simple UI for results.
concrete_steps: Define request/response format: GET /api/search/videos?q=...&page=...&limit=...&sort=....
Server: add a video search endpoint (SQLite FTS5, fallback to LIKE).

tasks.title: Docstrings for all modules and functions
tasks.problem: descriptions are missing in some places, making it harder to quickly understand module and function purpose.
tasks.solution_option: add short docstring descriptions to all modules and functions where they are missing.
concrete_steps: Modules: 2–5 lines, “what the module does / main responsibilities.”
Functions/classes: 1–2 lines, “what it does / what it returns.”
Avoid noise — only where it is non-obvious.

tasks.title: Popular: weighted random by similarity
tasks.problem: when likes exist, popular is sorted by similarity but then a random sample is taken from the whole pool, so the sorting almost does not affect the result.
tasks.solution_option: replace random.sample with weighted random using similarity as the weight.
concrete_steps: After sorting/scoring, compute weights (e.g., weight = max(similarity, 0.0) or weight = similarity alpha).
Pick candidates via weighted sampling without replacement (or with a small epsilon for diversity).
Add config parameter popular.weighted_random_alpha (0 = disabled).
For empty/zero weights fallback to normal random.

tasks.title: Crawler mode: start from one instance and its subscriptions
tasks.problem: there is no crawler mode that starts from one instance and walks its federated subscriptions.
tasks.solution_option: add a mode where a seed instance domain is provided, then the crawler:
concrete_steps: CLI parameter for the seed domain (e.g., --seed-instance).
Step 1: fetch subscriptions/follows of the instance and build a list of instances.
Step 2: iterate through channels of each instance and collect their metadata.
Step 3: iterate through videos of channels and save them to the DB.
Note: this mode belongs to the instance crawler; its behavior should be aligned with existing crawler flags.

tasks.title: Update recommendation description (RECOMMENDATIONS_OVERVIEW)
tasks.problem: RECOMMENDATIONS_OVERVIEW.md does not match the current recommendation logic.
tasks.solution_option: rewrite the document and describe the whole generation pipeline.
concrete_steps: Describe data preparation stages (embeddings, cache, index).
Describe candidate generation and layer mixing.
Describe filters, deduplication, and mixing rules.
Describe what the client receives and how the frontend uses it.

tasks.title: Background refresh of random cache
tasks.problem: random cache is rebuilt only on server start and/or on a refresh flag. We need a background mechanism with safe updates without read conflicts.
tasks.solution_option: periodic job that rebuilds the cache into a separate file and atomically swaps the active cache.
concrete_steps: Config: parameter RANDOM_CACHE_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MINUTES (0 = disabled).
Background thread/timer in the server that rebuilds every N minutes.
Rebuild into random-cache.tmp.db, then atomic swap (rename) and safe connection re-open under random_cache_lock.
Logs/metrics: build time, size, number of valid candidates, short-fill reasons.
Consider SQLite: use WAL/readonly connection for reading, update only under a lock for replacement.

tasks.title: Video page similars: diversity on refresh + larger candidate coverage
tasks.problem: on video page (/api/similar for upnext), refresh often returns the same small set; for some videos only 1-5 similars are found. We need stable diversity (with likes and without likes) and larger similar pools.
tasks.solution_option: expand ANN candidate recall (search_limit, top-k, nprobe), add deterministic shuffle/window sampling for final output, and add fallback broadening when candidate count is low.
concrete_steps: Config-driven ANN for video-page similars:
Add/verify dedicated config defaults for upnext retrieval (separate from home feed where needed):
SIMILAR_VIDEO_SEARCH_LIMIT
SIMILAR_VIDEO_TOP_K
SIMILAR_VIDEO_NPROBE
Keep these in server_config and expose in logs on startup.
Low-candidate fallback strategy:
If final unique candidates < target_min_pool:
increase ANN depth in steps (nprobe up to max),
increase search_limit in steps (bounded),
optionally relax min similarity threshold for tail fill.
Stop when pool is enough or hard caps reached.
Diversity per refresh (no repeated top-8):
Do not always take first N after sorting.
Use one of:
windowed sampling from top-M (e.g., random from top-200),
weighted random by similarity (without replacement),
deterministic daily seed + request nonce for controlled variation.
Keep quality by preserving similarity-weighted preference.
Behavior with likes vs without likes:
likes=yes: mix source-video similars with profile-aware candidates (but keep source-video relevance dominant).
likes=no: still diversify output from source-video pool (no profile dependency required).
Ensure both paths can produce varied output on refresh.
Dedup/quality guarantees:
Keep strict dedup by (video_uuid, instance_domain).
Keep existing blocked-instance/channel filters.
Enforce minimum relevance floor before fallback tail.
Observability:
Add logs per upnext request:
initial pool size,
fallback steps triggered,
final pool size,
sampling mode used,
number of unique items returned.
Validation / tests:
Repeated refresh test (same video, 10 requests): overlap ratio should be below threshold while relevance stays acceptable.
Low-similarity video test: verify fallback expands pool above minimum.
Compare behavior for likes=yes and likes=no.

tasks.title: Stable ANN IDs: replace video_embeddings.rowid with deterministic video_id+host -> int64
tasks.problem: ANN currently uses SQLite video_embeddings.rowid as FAISS id source. This is tightly coupled to physical row layout and becomes operationally fragile after purge/merge/rebuild cycles. We need stable ANN ids derived from logical video identity.
tasks.solution_option: introduce deterministic ANN id (ann_id) from canonical key (video_id, instance_domain) and migrate ANN/search/precompute pipelines to use it end-to-end.
concrete_steps: ID contract + helper module:
Add canonical key builder (video_id + "::" + normalized instance_domain).
Add deterministic int64 generator (stable hash) and collision policy.
Expose one source of truth helper for all writers/readers.
Schema + migration:
Add ann_id to video_embeddings (or dedicated mapping table) with unique index.
Backfill existing rows and validate no collisions.
Update schema migration jobs to guarantee ann_id exists before ANN build.
Embedding write paths:
Ensure every insert/update into video_embeddings writes deterministic ann_id.
Keep behavior idempotent for re-runs and replace merges.
ANN build path migration:
build-ann-index.py: read ann_id instead of rowid; add_with_ids(..., ann_id).
Update index metadata (id_source) to the new contract.
Runtime search path migration:
data/ann.py: ANN search returns ann_id list, not rowids.
data/embeddings.py: seed resolves exclude_ann_id instead of exclude_rowid.
data/metadata.py: add fetch-by-ann_id path and use it in ANN response handlers.
handlers/similar.py: vector search path should pass exclude_ann_id and resolve metadata by ann_id.
Similarity precompute migration:
precompute-similar-ann.py: switch internal ANN ids and metadata lookup from rowid to ann_id.
Incremental selection should remain logical-key based (video_id, instance_domain), not physical row ids.
Updater/ops integration:
Ensure updater sequence keeps ANN id consistency before ANN rebuild/precompute stages.
Update docs/runbooks to include ANN id migration/backfill step for existing DBs.
Validation / tests:
Unit test: deterministic id generation and collision guard.
Migration test: backfill creates stable ids and unique constraint holds.
Integration test: ANN response correctness after host purge (no rowid coupling).
Smoke test: updater full cycle (merge -> ANN build -> precompute) with new id source.
ANN runtime/search: server/data/ann.py, server/api/handlers/similar.py, server/data/embeddings.py, server/data/metadata.py.
ANN build/precompute: server/db/jobs/build-ann-index.py, server/db/jobs/precompute-similar-ann.py.
DB schema/migration/ingest: server/db/jobs/migrate-whitelist.py, server/db/jobs/build-video-embeddings.py, server/db/jobs/sync-whitelist.py, merge/updater flow.
Ops/docs/tests: server/db/jobs/updater-worker.py, updater docs, ANN/smoke tests.

tasks.title: Request lifecycle logs: request-start first + shared request_id across server logs
tasks.problem: current logs are hard to correlate because access logs are emitted on response write, while business logs ([similar-server], [recommendations]) are produced during processing and can interleave across threads.
tasks.solution_option: introduce a per-request lifecycle log (start -> work logs -> end) and one shared request_id propagated through handler and recommendation logs.
concrete_steps: Keep a two-log model (do not merge into one physical file):
nginx access log for client/network view (ip, URL, status, timing),
app/service log for internal processing (similar-server, recommendations, timing).
Correlation contract:
one shared request_id must appear in both logs for the same request;
nginx should forward X-Request-ID (or generate it when missing), app should read and reuse it.
Add a request-scoped context value (request_id) at the start of every API request (do_GET/do_POST) before business logic.
Emit request-start log first with request_id, client IP, method, full URL, and optional user-agent.
Reuse the same request_id in all downstream request-path logs ([similar-server], [recommendations], handler timing logs).
Emit request-end log with request_id, status, duration_ms; bytes in app-log is optional.
Treat response byte size as nginx access-log responsibility (source of truth for transferred bytes).
Remove ad-hoc per-handler random id generation where it conflicts with shared request context.
Keep compatibility with threaded serving (ordering guaranteed per request, not globally).
Validation:
smoke test for /api/similar, /api/video, /api/health to verify start -> ... -> end with same request_id;
regression test to ensure no request is logged without request_id;
manual verification runbook that compares one request in both logs by request_id.

tasks.title: Random cache refresh worker without startup downtime (extends 16l runtime behavior)
tasks.problem: rebuilding random cache during startup can delay readiness and create a visible unavailable window after restart.
tasks.solution_option: keep serving from existing cache immediately, and rebuild cache in a background worker with atomic swap.
concrete_steps: Keep startup non-blocking:
open existing random-cache.db and start API listening first;
if cache is missing/invalid, use safe DB fallback until first successful build.
Add background worker/timer to rebuild into random-cache.tmp.db.
Perform atomic cache swap and safe connection reopen under random_cache_lock.
Add backoff/retry policy for failed refreshes; do not block request path.
Add metrics logs: build duration, scanned rows, final size, short-fill reasons, swap success/failure.
Config:
refresh interval (minutes),
startup refresh mode (off / async),
optional max build runtime guard.
Validation:
startup readiness test confirms /api/health responds before cache rebuild completes;
concurrent read test during swap shows no request failures.

tasks.title: Zero-downtime server deploy: parallel port startup + automatic nginx switch
tasks.problem: in-place restart on one port causes temporary downtime during server initialization/warm-up.
tasks.solution_option: implement blue/green style deploy for the API: start a new instance on a second port, health-check it, switch nginx upstream, then drain/stop old instance.
concrete_steps: Add one-command deploy script (example name: deploy-bluegreen.sh) that performs full switch automatically.
Add explicit blue/green mode flag in the script (example: --blue-green).
Use a fixed blue/green port pair only: 7070 and 7071 (no random/free-port selection and no custom port list flag).
Move API service to systemd instance template (for example peertube-browser@.service) so two instances can run in parallel:
peertube-browser@7070,
peertube-browser@7071.
Deploy flow in script:
Add automatic rollback:
if readiness check fails, stop new instance and keep old port active;
if nginx validation/reload fails, restore previous upstream target and keep old instance.
Keep deploy idempotent and lock-protected (avoid concurrent deploy races).
Add operation logs: deploy id, old/new port, switch timestamp, health-check result, rollback reason.
Validation:
repeated deploy smoke tests with no 5xx spikes in nginx access log;
forced-failure test verifies rollback path;
verify one-command run requires no manual systemctl/nginx edits.

tasks.title: Timestamped request logs (explicit date/time)
tasks.problem: request timing analysis is harder when logs rely only on journal envelope time or inconsistent message formatting.
tasks.solution_option: include explicit timestamp in application log format and in request lifecycle logs.
concrete_steps: Configure logging format with explicit timestamp (recommended: ISO-8601 with milliseconds, UTC).
Ensure both request lifecycle logs and internal server logs include the same timestamp format.
Add optional config for structured log output (plain text default, JSON optional).
Keep compatibility with journald (no duplicate parsing assumptions).
Validation:
sample log lines include full date/time and timezone marker;
ordering checks across request-start/work/request-end use application timestamp fields.

tasks.title: Static page visit logs for About and informational pages
tasks.problem: visits to static informational pages (including about) are currently served as static files by nginx, so these page visits are not visible in app/service request logs.
tasks.solution_option: add a dedicated logging path for static page visits and keep correlation with request tracing where possible.
concrete_steps: Add dedicated nginx logging for informational static page routes:
include client IP, method, URL, status, response time, user-agent;
keep this in a separate log stream or with explicit marker field.
Preserve/propagate X-Request-ID in nginx logs for those routes when available.
Add a simple runbook to compare static page visits from nginx logs and API request traces from app logs.
Optional (if needed): add client-side pageview beacon endpoint for cleaner human-intent tracking and bot/noise filtering.
Validation:
visiting informational static pages produces entries with expected fields in nginx logs;
sample correlation by request id/time window works against app-side traces.

tasks.title: Similarity cache shadow build + atomic swap without long API downtime
tasks.problem: updater currently keeps API service stopped until precompute-similar-ann.py finishes. Similarity precompute can be very long, causing unnecessary API downtime.
tasks.solution_option: build similarity cache in a shadow DB while API is already running, then perform a fast atomic cutover.
concrete_steps: Updater sequence change:
keep service stop/start around write-conflict-critical stages only (merge + ANN rebuild),
start API service before similarity precompute stage.
Shadow cache build path:
create similarity-cache.next.db from current active similarity-cache.db (preserve incremental baseline),
run precompute-similar-ann.py --incremental against the shadow file, not active file.
Atomic cutover:
on success, atomically replace active cache file (os.replace),
keep rollback backup (similarity-cache.prev.db) for one-step restore.
Runtime handoff:
add safe similarity DB reconnect/reopen path in API process under similarity_db_lock (signal or admin hook),
fallback: short controlled API restart only for reconnect if hot-reload is unavailable.
Safety/rollback:
if shadow build fails, keep active cache untouched,
if swap/reopen fails, restore previous active cache and keep service healthy.
Observability:
log shadow build duration, processed sources, swap duration, reopen result, rollback reason.
Validation / tests:
integration test: API stays available during similarity precompute window,
concurrency test: no read errors during swap/reopen,
rollback test: forced swap failure restores previous cache and keeps /api/health green.

tasks.title: Similarity precompute: rewrite only existing cache sources
tasks.problem: updater currently runs similarity precompute over all embeddings with full cache recreation, which is heavy and can keep service downtime longer than needed.
tasks.solution_option: add a mode that recomputes similarity only for source videos already present in similarity_sources, and rewrites only those cache entries.
concrete_steps: Add dedicated CLI mode in precompute-similar-ann.py:
source set = intersection of current embeddings and existing similarity_sources in output cache DB;
process only this set instead of all embeddings.
For each processed source, keep current rewrite semantics:
upsert similarity_sources.computed_at,
delete old similarity_items for this source,
insert fresh top-k rows.
Keep non-processed source entries untouched (no global cache wipe).
Updater integration:
use the new mode for updater precompute stage,
stop passing full-reset behavior intended for complete rebuilds.
Validation / tests:
source-count check: processed set equals “already cached + still present in embeddings”;
data check: processed sources are rewritten, untouched sources remain unchanged;
runtime check: updater stage time drops compared to full-cache rebuild baseline.

tasks.title: Workflow CLI entrypoint and command routing
tasks.problem: workflow operations are still tied to manual file edits and ad-hoc command handling.
tasks.solution_option: add one dev/workflow entrypoint with stable routing for feature/task/confirm/validate command groups.
concrete_steps: Create dev/workflow CLI entrypoint with argument parsing and subcommand dispatch.
Implement command router modules for feature, task, confirm, and validate.
Add smoke checks that verify command resolution and non-zero exit code on invalid arguments.

tasks.title: Feature base commands: create/plan/approve/execution-plan
tasks.problem: feature lifecycle commands are not unified under one executable contract.
tasks.solution_option: implement base feature workflow commands required before sync/materialize.
concrete_steps: Implement feature create with feature ID/milestone validation and tracker update wiring.
Implement feature plan-init, feature plan-lint, and feature approve with plan/gate checks.
Implement feature execution-plan that returns ordered pending tasks for one feature subtree.

tasks.title: Tracking sync command for DEV_MAP/TASK_LIST/PIPELINE
tasks.problem: local decomposition sync across trackers is manual and error-prone.
tasks.solution_option: implement plan tasks for feature --write for one-change-set updates of all tracking files.
concrete_steps: Implement sync input model for manual decomposition delta (Issue -> Task, markers, pipeline order/overlaps/outcome).
Implement write path that updates dev/map/DEV_MAP.json, dev/TASK_LIST.md, and dev/TASK_EXECUTION_PIPELINE.md together.
Add guard that blocks write when feature status is not Approved.

tasks.title: Task ID allocation and validation scopes
tasks.problem: ID allocation and cross-tracker consistency checks are not enforced by automation.
tasks.solution_option: add deterministic task_count allocation and repository/tracking validators.
concrete_steps: Implement task ID allocation strictly via task_count (new_id = task_count + 1) and persist in the same write run.
Implement ownership validation for [M*][F*]/[M*][SI*] markers against DEV_MAP parent chains.
Implement validate --scope tracking|repo checks for sync consistency and gate failures.

tasks.title: Add issue-level Approved transition
tasks.problem: issue lifecycle lacks approved gate and jumps from planned directly to done.
tasks.solution_option: extend confirm contract with explicit approved transition for issue nodes.
concrete_steps: Allow confirm issue approved state in CLI parser and handlers.
Persist issue status Planned to Approved in DEV_MAP.
Return deterministic output fields for approved transitions.

tasks.title: Gate execute and materialize by issue Approved status
tasks.problem: execute issue and materialize issue modes currently run without issue-level approval gate.
tasks.solution_option: require issue status Approved before execute issue and issues-create or issues-sync materialize paths.
concrete_steps: Add status gate checks in task execution and materialize issue mode handlers.
Return clear gate error messages with required status.
Add regression checks for blocked non-approved issue paths.

tasks.title: Add Draft planning status lifecycle to tracking helpers
tasks.problem: the current planning lifecycle only distinguishes Pending and Planned, which forces planning logic to infer draft state indirectly from FEATURE_PLANS content.
tasks.solution_option: introduce explicit Pending, Draft, Planned, and Tasked lifecycle semantics in DEV_MAP-facing helpers so planning and validation commands operate on status alone.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit the status checks and transitions currently hard-coded in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py, especially ISSUE_PLANNING_ACTIVE_STATUSES, ISSUE_ALLOWED_PLANNING_STATUSES, _enforce_plan_issue_order_row_presence, _collect_issue_planning_status_mismatches, and _reconcile_feature_issue_planning_statuses.

2. Replace Pending or Planned-only planning assumptions in those code paths with explicit Draft-aware handling, and update any related DEV_MAP mutation logic so Draft can exist without changing downstream Tasked execution semantics.
3. Validate that plan lint, plan-issue gating, and plan tasks status checks now read planning state from DEV_MAP status directly instead of inferring draft existence only from FEATURE_PLANS blocks.

tasks.title: Implement validate issue command and Gate 0 checks
tasks.problem: issue plans can be written into FEATURE_PLANS but there is no explicit command that validates them and promotes the issue from Draft to Planned.
tasks.solution_option: add validate issue as the canonical Gate 0 approval command with deterministic block checks, lifecycle transition rules, and actionable failures.
concrete_steps: 1. Extend dev/workflow_lib/cli.py and dev/workflow_lib/validate_commands.py with a canonical issue-scoped command surface instead of only `validate --scope ...`, including parser wiring for issue ID, dry-run, and write-mode behavior.

2. Reuse the FEATURE_PLANS parsing logic already present in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py to read one issue block, then add Gate 0 checks for required headings, numbered decomposition steps, and issue/task assessment content.
3. Promote only Draft issues to Planned in DEV_MAP on successful write-mode validation and return deterministic JSON for success, blocked status, missing issue block, and malformed plan content.

tasks.title: Implement validate feature cascade for child issue plans
tasks.problem: reviewing a whole feature plan still requires ad hoc per-issue checks, and there is no explicit feature-level validation gate for drafted child plans.
tasks.solution_option: add validate feature so one command can validate all child issue plan blocks, promote valid children, and keep parent feature status aligned.
concrete_steps: 1. Add feature-scoped validation routing in dev/workflow_lib/validate_commands.py that resolves one feature, iterates its child issues from DEV_MAP, and reads each corresponding issue block from FEATURE_PLANS.
2. Extract the issue-block Gate 0 checks into one shared validator used by both validate issue and validate feature so FEATURE_PLANS parsing and heading validation are not duplicated.
3. Implement write-mode promotion for validated child issues and the parent feature, and return deterministic payloads for partial-invalid child sets without silently promoting broken plans.

tasks.title: Cover Draft lifecycle and validation transitions with regression tests
tasks.problem: planning-state refactors can silently break status transitions or validation contracts if they are not locked by tests.
tasks.solution_option: add regression coverage for Pending, Draft, Planned, and feature-level validation paths before later command-surface changes build on them.
concrete_steps: 1. Add tests covering Draft-aware status handling in the feature planning helpers and in validate command routing so Pending, Draft, Planned, and Tasked states are all exercised.
2. Add command-level tests for validate issue and validate feature in dry-run and write modes, including missing block, malformed block, and blocked-status paths.
3. Verify in tests that only the intended DEV_MAP nodes change status on success and that invalid FEATURE_PLANS content never promotes issue or feature status.

tasks.title: Add --input draft-file support to plan issue parser
tasks.problem: plan issue still depends on inline arguments, which makes multiline agent-generated drafts harder to pass safely and consistently.
tasks.solution_option: extend the canonical plan issue parser with optional markdown draft-file input while preserving explicit arg mode for direct planning runs.
concrete_steps: 1. Extend the existing `feature plan-issue` parser in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py with an optional `--input` path and keep the current explicit flags available for non-file planning flows.
2. Route `--input` through parse_feature_issue_template from dev/workflow_lib/markdown_parser.py and reject mixed file-plus-flag input before any FEATURE_PLANS mutation happens.
3. Normalize parsed and explicit values into one payload shape so `_handle_feature_plan_issue` and `_upsert_issue_plan_block_in_section` can use the same downstream write path.

tasks.title: Write plan issue output as Draft status with deterministic updates
tasks.problem: planning currently lacks one clear write path that both updates FEATURE_PLANS and records the issue as Draft without implicitly approving it.
tasks.solution_option: route plan issue writes through one Draft-producing path that creates or updates the issue block and leaves promotion to validate commands.
concrete_steps: 1. Update `_handle_feature_plan_issue` in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so parsed draft-file values and explicit CLI values both flow through `_upsert_issue_plan_block_in_section` and one scoped lint path.

2. After a successful write, update the owning issue node in DEV_MAP from Pending to Draft, preserve Draft on later updates, and avoid changing already Planned issues unless an explicit overwrite rule is introduced.
3. Return deterministic JSON that distinguishes created-versus-updated plan blocks and reports Draft as the resulting planning state instead of silently promoting to Planned.

tasks.title: Add state guards for Pending, Draft, and Planned issue planning flows
tasks.problem: without explicit guards, repeated planning can silently downgrade Planned issues or produce ambiguous rewrites across lifecycle states.
tasks.solution_option: add state-aware guardrails to plan issue so Draft and Planned nodes are handled explicitly and unsafe rewrites are blocked by default.
concrete_steps: 1. Reject conflicting `--input` plus inline-flag usage and malformed draft-file input before `_handle_feature_plan_issue` mutates FEATURE_PLANS or DEV_MAP.

2. Add explicit state guards in the plan-issue write path so Pending can become Draft, Draft can be updated in place, and Planned cannot be silently downgraded or overwritten without an explicit contract.
3. Return deterministic WorkflowCommandError messages for blocked rewrites, bad feature assertions, and lifecycle downgrade attempts.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for plan issue input and Draft behavior
tasks.problem: input-mode changes and Draft-state logic can drift quickly without command-level regression coverage.
tasks.solution_option: add tests that lock draft-file parsing, explicit-arg planning, lifecycle transitions, and invalid rewrite behavior for plan issue.
concrete_steps: 1. Add tests that verify draft-file input and explicit CLI input produce the same canonical issue block under the parent feature section in FEATURE_PLANS.
2. Add lifecycle tests for Pending-to-Draft writes, Draft-in-place updates, and guarded Planned behavior in DEV_MAP.
3. Add failure-path coverage for mixed input modes, parser failures from dev/workflow_lib/markdown_parser.py, and blocked downgrade or overwrite attempts.

tasks.title: Audit legacy feature plan-issue routing and planning call sites
tasks.problem: legacy feature plan-issue wiring is still scattered across runtime and documentation references, which makes canonical command migration risky.
tasks.solution_option: catalog every remaining legacy registration, help string, and call site before replacing them with top-level plan and status commands.
concrete_steps: 1. Trace the existing `feature plan-issue` parser registration in register_feature_router, its handler `_handle_feature_plan_issue`, and every runtime message that still emits `feature.plan-issue` as the command name.

2. Audit docs and workflows that still mention feature-owned issue planning, especially .agents/workflows/plan-issue.md, .agents/workflows/plan-feature.md, and any help text emitted through dev/workflow_lib/cli.py.
3. Record which code paths must move to top-level `plan issue` and which old entrypoints must become explicit rejection paths instead of hidden compatibility aliases.

tasks.title: Promote plan issue to the canonical top-level planning entrypoint
tasks.problem: issue planning remains tied to a feature-owned legacy namespace, which makes the command surface harder to discover and contradicts the planned lifecycle model.
tasks.solution_option: move issue planning to canonical top-level plan issue routing and delete the legacy feature-owned registration from runtime code.
concrete_steps: 1. Add top-level `plan issue` routing through dev/workflow_lib/cli.py and register_plan_router in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so issue planning is no longer hidden under the feature router.
2. Remove the `plan-issue` subparser from register_feature_router and stop emitting `feature.plan-issue` from `_handle_feature_plan_issue` output payloads.
3. Update command help and error text so canonical issue planning is discoverable through `plan issue` and the legacy `feature plan-issue` path is rejected explicitly.

tasks.title: Implement compact status issue and status feature commands
tasks.problem: agents currently need to inspect large tracker files or verbose command output to learn one issue or feature status, which wastes tokens and complicates automation.
tasks.solution_option: add minimal JSON status commands for issue and feature nodes so lifecycle polling is cheap and deterministic.
concrete_steps: 1. Extend dev/workflow_lib/cli.py with a new top-level status router and implement feature or issue resolution against DEV_MAP instead of reusing the broad `validate --scope ...` surface.
2. Return minimal JSON from the new handlers with only command name, resolved ID, and status so automation does not need to parse full tracker payloads.
3. Keep blocked-ID and not-found behavior deterministic so status polling can be used safely by later planning and execution workflows.

tasks.title: Add routing tests for canonical status/plan commands and legacy rejection
tasks.problem: CLI migration can leave hidden routing regressions or stale legacy acceptance unless command-surface behavior is tested directly.
tasks.solution_option: add smoke and regression checks that cover canonical plan and status commands while explicitly rejecting feature plan-issue.
concrete_steps: 1. Add tests for top-level `plan issue`, `status issue`, and `status feature` routing through dev/workflow_lib/cli.py and their expected compact JSON payloads.
2. Add explicit rejection tests for `feature plan-issue` so the old parser path cannot survive as an undocumented compatibility surface.
3. Verify help text and command dispatch stay aligned with the new planning and status command tree.

tasks.title: Rewrite planning protocol docs around Draft validation lifecycle
tasks.problem: protocol docs still describe the older planning lifecycle and do not document Draft status, compact status commands, or feature-level validation gates consistently.
tasks.solution_option: rewrite planning and execution protocol sections so lifecycle wording and command gates match the intended Draft validation model.
concrete_steps: 1. Update .agents/protocols/feature-planning-protocol.md and .agents/protocols/task-execution-protocol.md so lifecycle wording matches the Draft-aware runtime contract.
2. Add explicit validate issue, validate feature, and status issue or feature guidance where planning and execution gates are defined.
3. Remove wording that still implies draft existence must be inferred only from FEATURE_PLANS content instead of DEV_MAP status and validate commands.

tasks.title: Update plan-feature workflow for cascading draft planning behavior
tasks.problem: the current feature planning workflow still risks implying feature-only planning or unsafe overwrite behavior for already drafted child issues.
tasks.solution_option: rewrite plan-feature so it remains a valid cascading planning workflow that drafts only missing child plans and respects existing Draft or Planned work.
concrete_steps: 1. Rewrite .agents/workflows/plan-feature.md so its phases reflect the real feature planning flow implemented around FEATURE_PLANS and DEV_MAP.
2. Document how the workflow should treat existing Draft and Planned child issue plans without blindly overwriting them during feature replanning.
3. Add the follow-up validation step using validate issue or validate feature so the workflow matches the new runtime contract.

tasks.title: Update plan-issue workflow to canonical plan and validate flow
tasks.problem: plan-issue workflow text still depends on legacy feature-owned command naming and does not clearly document the Draft-to-Planned transition.
tasks.solution_option: rewrite plan-issue around canonical plan issue and validate issue steps so issue drafting and approval are separated clearly.
concrete_steps: 1. Rewrite .agents/workflows/plan-issue.md so its execute step uses canonical `plan issue` commands instead of feature-owned issue planning syntax.
2. Document Draft as the result of the plan-issue write path and Planned as the result of later validate issue or validate feature promotion.
3. Add compact status-check guidance before and after validation so the workflow matches the intended `status issue` command surface.

tasks.title: Add consistency checks for lifecycle docs and canonical planning commands
tasks.problem: protocol and workflow docs can drift apart from runtime planning commands, leaving contradictory guidance around Draft lifecycle and legacy namespaces.
tasks.solution_option: add consistency checks that catch stale feature-only planning claims and leftover feature plan-issue references after the lifecycle rewrite.
concrete_steps: 1. Add grep-style or parser-level checks that fail when canonical docs still mention `feature plan-issue` after the routing cleanup.
2. Verify canonical workflow and protocol docs no longer claim that plan feature is feature-only when child issue drafting is part of the runtime lifecycle.
3. Keep command examples, lifecycle wording, and status terminology aligned across docs after the Draft validation rewrite.

tasks.title: Audit current confirm feature cleanup and closure gates
tasks.problem: confirm feature currently updates trackers and closes issues without one clearly documented contract for when full feature-plan cleanup is allowed.
tasks.solution_option: trace existing confirm feature and confirm issue behavior across FEATURE_PLANS, TASK_LIST, PIPELINE, and GitHub close flows before tightening closure rules.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit `_handle_confirm_feature_done`, `_handle_confirm_issue_done`, and `_handle_confirm_issues_done` in dev/workflow_lib/confirm_commands.py, including how they branch between dry-run and write mode.
2. Trace the concrete cleanup helpers used for FEATURE_PLANS, TASK_LIST, TASK_EXECUTION_PIPELINE, DEV_MAP status writes, gh_issue_edit_body, and close_github_issue.
3. Record exactly where feature-level confirm diverges from issue-level cleanup, especially around subtree gating, FEATURE_PLANS section removal, and GitHub close behavior.

tasks.title: Define strict confirm feature closure contract
tasks.problem: without one strict non-cascading contract, confirm feature can remain ambiguous about whether it is allowed to close unfinished child issues or remove feature plan artifacts.
tasks.solution_option: define confirm feature done as a strict path that succeeds only when all child issues are already Done and previews all cleanup side effects before write mode.
concrete_steps: 1. Define the exact preconditions inside `_handle_confirm_feature_done` for the default path, including which child issue statuses are allowed and how repeat confirm runs should behave.
2. Add explicit preview fields for whole-feature FEATURE_PLANS cleanup alongside the existing tracker cleanup payload returned from confirm commands.
3. Keep the default feature-confirm path non-cascading in parser and handler semantics so any subtree-closing behavior is introduced only through a separate explicit flag.

tasks.title: Implement feature-level FEATURE_PLANS cleanup on valid confirm
tasks.problem: feature confirmation currently leaves the parent feature section in FEATURE_PLANS even when closure is valid, which leaves planning artifacts behind after completion.
tasks.solution_option: add feature-section removal to confirm feature when the strict closure preconditions are satisfied and keep dry-run and write behavior deterministic.
concrete_steps: 1. Add a helper in dev/workflow_lib/confirm_commands.py that removes the full `## <feature_id>` section from dev/FEATURE_PLANS.md only after strict feature-close preconditions are satisfied.
2. Call that helper from `_handle_confirm_feature_done` in the same write sequence that applies tracker cleanup, rather than adding a separate post-step.
3. Return explicit preview and applied payload fields showing whether the full feature section would be removed or was removed.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for strict confirm feature cleanup
tasks.problem: cleanup and closure-gate changes can silently regress subtree safety or idempotent confirm behavior unless they are locked by tests.
tasks.solution_option: add regression checks that cover blocked, successful, and repeat confirm feature flows under the new strict cleanup contract.
concrete_steps: 1. Add tests covering blocked `_handle_confirm_feature_done` runs when any child issue is not Done.
2. Add tests for successful write-mode feature confirmation that removes the whole feature section from FEATURE_PLANS and keeps other tracker cleanup aligned.
3. Add repeat-run tests so feature confirmation remains idempotent after the feature section has already been removed.

tasks.title: Define explicit child-issue cascade command surface
tasks.problem: users need one intentional way to close a feature subtree in one command, but overloading generic force semantics would make closure side effects too opaque.
tasks.solution_option: define one explicit feature confirm flag that clearly communicates child-issue and child-task cascade behavior and keeps it separate from the strict default path.
concrete_steps: 1. Replace or complement the current generic `--force` surface in register_confirm_router with one explicit cascade flag name that clearly targets child issues and child tasks.
2. Define in confirm command parsing and payload semantics exactly which child-task, child-issue, feature cleanup, and GitHub close actions happen in cascade mode.
3. Keep reporting explicit for already-Done children, unmapped GitHub children, and mixed subtree states so the cascade surface stays distinct from the strict default path.

tasks.title: Implement subtree cascade executor for confirm feature
tasks.problem: there is no explicit runtime path that reuses issue-level cleanup semantics to close an entire feature subtree in one controlled write run.
tasks.solution_option: implement a feature-level cascade executor that confirms child issues and tasks, removes their plan artifacts, and closes mapped GitHub issues via one explicit mode.
concrete_steps: 1. Refactor `_handle_confirm_feature_done` to reuse `_cleanup_feature_plan_issue_artifacts`, tracker cleanup helpers, and child-task completion logic already used by `_handle_confirm_issue_done`.
2. Close mapped child GitHub issues and the parent feature GitHub issue through the same controlled cascade path instead of a second independent close sequence.
3. Return deterministic cascade payloads for mixed subtree states, already-Done children, and unmapped GitHub children.

tasks.title: Update confirm workflow guidance for strict versus cascade modes
tasks.problem: once subtree cascade exists, users need clear documentation that explains how it differs from the strict default confirm feature path.
tasks.solution_option: rewrite confirm workflow guidance and examples so strict and cascade confirm modes are discoverable and their side effects are obvious.
concrete_steps: 1. Update .agents/workflows/confirm.md and confirm help output so the strict feature-confirm path and the explicit child-issue cascade path are shown as separate commands.
2. Document which FEATURE_PLANS, DEV_MAP, TASK_LIST, TASK_EXECUTION_PIPELINE, and GitHub close actions happen only in cascade mode.
3. Keep the docs aligned with the exact flag name and payload fields implemented in dev/workflow_lib/confirm_commands.py.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for child-issue cascade confirm mode
tasks.problem: feature-level cascade behavior can easily become unsafe or ambiguous without direct dry-run and write-mode regression coverage.
tasks.solution_option: add tests that lock cascade previews, subtree completion behavior, and edge-case reporting for the explicit child-issue cascade mode.
concrete_steps: 1. Add dry-run tests that preview the exact child issues, tasks, FEATURE_PLANS artifacts, and GitHub issues touched by cascade mode.
2. Add write-mode tests for successful subtree cascade across mixed unfinished child issues and tasks using the shared cleanup helpers.
3. Add edge-case tests for already-Done children and partially unmapped GitHub issue sets so reporting stays deterministic.

tasks.title: Define issue-scoped execution commit policy contract
tasks.problem: execution commit behavior is currently ad hoc, so users do not know whether execute issue should create commits automatically or what message format should be considered canonical.
tasks.solution_option: define one issue-scoped commit policy that decides whether commit creation is optional, explicit-flag based, or documentation-only and standardizes the message format.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit the current execute-issue workflow and task execution path to determine whether commit creation belongs in runtime code or in workflow guidance only.
2. Define one canonical commit message format that references the issue identity and, if available, the mapped GitHub issue number without conflating it with confirm semantics.
3. Record the commit-policy decision in a way that stays independent from confirm subtree cleanup behavior in dev/workflow_lib/confirm_commands.py.

tasks.title: Evaluate runtime integration points for explicit issue commits
tasks.problem: automatic commits can hide repository mutations from the user if they are introduced without a careful integration review of execute issue flows.
tasks.solution_option: inspect execute issue integration points and make a defensible decision about whether explicit commit automation is safe enough for runtime support.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit the current execute-issue workflow docs and task execution path to find where an explicit commit hook could run without hiding repository mutations from the user.
2. Decide whether any runtime hook belongs in CLI code or whether commit behavior should remain an explicit documented manual step.
3. If runtime support is warranted, define explicit flag gating, failure behavior, and safe interaction with dirty worktrees before any commit command is added.

tasks.title: Document GitHub issue and feature visibility limits for linked commits
tasks.problem: users may assume child-issue-linked commits automatically appear as a rich aggregated stream on the parent feature issue, which GitHub does not reliably provide.
tasks.solution_option: document the realistic visibility limits for commit references, closing keywords, child issues, and parent feature issues so execution expectations stay accurate.
concrete_steps: 1. Document in workflow guidance what commit references and closing keywords affect on child GitHub issues versus parent feature issues.
2. Clarify that parent-child issue hierarchy and linked PR behavior are separate from commit aggregation and may not surface child commits on the feature issue.
3. Keep the final workflow text explicit about the limits of parent-feature visibility for child-issue-linked commits.

tasks.title: Add coverage or checks for the chosen commit policy behavior
tasks.problem: once a commit policy is chosen, runtime and documentation can drift unless the selected behavior is covered by tests or consistency checks.
tasks.solution_option: add runtime tests if commit automation is implemented or documentation consistency checks if the policy remains workflow guidance only.
concrete_steps: 1. If runtime commit support is added, add command-level tests for the explicit flag surface and its blocked-state behavior.
2. Otherwise add documentation consistency checks that keep the chosen commit policy and message format aligned across execute workflows and related guidance.
3. Verify the repository docs never promise unsupported parent-feature GitHub commit aggregation behavior.

tasks.title: Audit current feature issue creation and materialize semantics
tasks.problem: feature-level GitHub issue creation is currently hidden behind ambiguous materialize-oriented semantics, so the explicit publish contract cannot be introduced safely without first tracing the existing behavior.
tasks.solution_option: catalog the current feature issue create and sync paths, milestone handling, and local mapping side effects before renaming the command surface around publish semantics.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit the current feature-level GitHub issue creation path in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py, especially `_handle_materialize_feature_action`, milestone resolution, and mapping updates.
2. Trace how ensure_github_milestone_exists, resolve_github_repository, gh_issue_create, and repeat-run skip logic are currently wired for feature issue publication.
3. Separate true feature-issue publication behavior from branch linkage and other materialize-oriented concerns so the new publish surface does not inherit unrelated side effects.

tasks.title: Define publish feature contract and output semantics
tasks.problem: without one explicit contract, publish feature could inherit hidden materialize semantics and remain ambiguous about whether it creates, syncs, or skips feature issues.
tasks.solution_option: define publish feature as the explicit feature issue publication contract with deterministic create-only behavior, already-published handling, and milestone ownership rules.
concrete_steps: 1. Define publish feature semantics for unpublished, already-published, and invalid feature targets around the current materialize feature implementation.
2. Specify dry-run versus write output fields that explicitly report GitHub issue publication rather than abstract materialization.
3. Keep milestone assignment implicit through DEV_MAP ownership and ensure_github_milestone_exists instead of adding redundant CLI input.

tasks.title: Implement publish feature runtime path
tasks.problem: the runtime still exposes feature issue creation through materialize-oriented behavior, which makes command intent harder to read and weakens the local-versus-remote lifecycle split.
tasks.solution_option: add an explicit publish feature runtime path that creates the feature GitHub issue and records local mapping state with publish-oriented messaging.
concrete_steps: 1. Add publish-oriented parser routing in dev/workflow_lib/cli.py and bind it to the existing feature-issue creation primitives in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py.
2. Rename runtime messaging and command semantics around feature issue creation without pulling branch-linkage behavior into the publish command contract.
3. Preserve deterministic local mapping updates and already-published repeat behavior during the surface rename.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for publish feature behavior
tasks.problem: renaming the feature issue creation surface can silently regress dry-run output, milestone assignment, or repeat-run behavior unless it is covered directly by tests.
tasks.solution_option: add regression coverage for publish feature preview, first publication, and already-published repeat paths.
concrete_steps: 1. Add dry-run tests for unpublished feature publication through the new publish surface.
2. Add write-mode tests for first-time feature issue creation, milestone resolution, and DEV_MAP mapping persistence.
3. Add repeat-run tests for already-published feature issues so the renamed command keeps deterministic skip behavior.

tasks.title: Define publish issue and publish issues batch contracts
tasks.problem: child issue publication is currently tied to materialize wording and mixed feature-owned semantics, which makes it unclear how single-issue and feature-owned batch publication should behave.
tasks.solution_option: define explicit contracts for publish issue and publish issues --feature-id with deterministic create, skip, error, and parent-feature linkage behavior.
concrete_steps: 1. Define one explicit contract for publishing a single child issue and one for publishing all child issues owned by one feature through the current issue materialization engine.
2. Specify how already-published child issues, invalid owner scopes, and missing parent feature issue mappings are reported.
3. Keep milestone lineage and parent-feature linkage expectations explicit around gh_issue_create and gh_issue_add_sub_issue behavior.

tasks.title: Implement publish issue runtime path for one child issue
tasks.problem: there is no explicit single-command surface that clearly says it publishes one issue to GitHub while preserving local milestone ownership and mapping rules.
tasks.solution_option: implement publish issue as the canonical single-child-issue publication command with deterministic mapping updates and error handling.
concrete_steps: 1. Add publish-oriented routing for one child issue on top of the existing single-issue materialization path in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py.
2. Preserve milestone ownership from DEV_MAP and ensure_github_milestone_exists without requiring extra CLI milestone input.
3. Return deterministic created, skipped, and blocked payloads for one selected issue publication.

tasks.title: Implement publish issues runtime path for feature-owned issue batches
tasks.problem: publishing all child issues of a feature still depends on materialize-oriented behavior, which obscures batch scope and makes parent-feature linkage semantics harder to reason about.
tasks.solution_option: implement publish issues --feature-id as an explicit feature-owned issue-batch publication flow with predictable queue order and linkage behavior.
concrete_steps: 1. Add publish-oriented batch routing that selects child issues by feature ownership using the existing issue-set materialization logic.
2. Keep milestone assignment, queue ordering, and already-published skip behavior deterministic across the selected batch.
3. Define and implement how parent feature issue presence affects gh_issue_add_sub_issue linkage and batch reporting.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for child issue publish flows
tasks.problem: single-issue and batch publication flows can easily drift on create, skip, and parent-linkage behavior unless they are covered by direct regression tests.
tasks.solution_option: add command-level coverage for publish issue and publish issues batch behavior across normal and edge-case publication paths.
concrete_steps: 1. Add success tests for one child issue publish and feature-owned issue-batch publish through the renamed command surface.
2. Add edge-case tests for already-published child issues, missing parent feature issue mappings, invalid scope selection, and sub-issue linkage behavior.
3. Verify deterministic output fields for created, skipped, selected, and blocked issue publication behavior.

tasks.title: Rewrite CLI help and examples around publish commands
tasks.problem: users currently see materialize-oriented examples that do not clearly communicate which commands create GitHub issues and which ones only manipulate local tracker state.
tasks.solution_option: rewrite CLI help and examples so publish feature, publish issue, and publish issues directly communicate GitHub publication side effects.
concrete_steps: 1. Rewrite CLI help in dev/workflow_lib/cli.py and related parser descriptions so feature and issue GitHub creation examples use publish-oriented wording instead of materialize wording.
2. Make the local-registration versus remote-publication split explicit in parser descriptions and examples.
3. Keep help output compact while making the publish model discoverable without reading runtime internals.

tasks.title: Update workflow docs for local registration versus GitHub publication
tasks.problem: workflow docs still describe local creation and remote GitHub issue creation through materialize language, which leaves the intended publication lifecycle unclear.
tasks.solution_option: rewrite workflow docs so local create flows and remote publish flows are clearly separated with milestone and parent/child publication guidance.
concrete_steps: 1. Update workflow docs that currently describe local registration and GitHub creation through materialize language so they describe create and publish as separate lifecycle steps.
2. Document milestone behavior and parent-feature versus child-issue publication paths in publish terminology.
3. Keep operator guidance aligned with the exact runtime command surfaces and output contracts.

tasks.title: Clean up legacy materialize wording and migration guidance
tasks.problem: if materialize remains the canonical wording in runtime or docs, the repository will expose two competing mental models for GitHub issue creation.
tasks.solution_option: remove materialize as the canonical publish path from runtime guidance and documentation while keeping any explicit migration notes accurate.
concrete_steps: 1. Replace remaining canonical materialize wording for GitHub issue creation in runtime messages and repository docs with publish-oriented terminology.
2. Keep migration or compatibility notes explicit instead of leaving stale materialize examples in canonical guidance.
3. Align runtime messages, workflows, and feature plans on one publish-oriented model.

tasks.title: Add consistency checks for publish help and docs surfaces
tasks.problem: help output and workflow docs can drift back toward materialize wording unless the new publish-oriented command surface is covered by explicit checks.
tasks.solution_option: add consistency checks that keep help output and workflow documentation aligned on publish feature, publish issue, and publish issues commands.
concrete_steps: 1. Add checks that compare documented examples and help output against the canonical publish commands exposed in the parser tree.
2. Verify no canonical docs still present materialize as the primary way to create GitHub issues.
3. Keep local-registration versus remote-publication terminology aligned across help text and workflow docs.

tasks.title: Define canonical milestone execution-order block format
tasks.problem: FEATURE_PLANS currently has no authoritative milestone-level execution-order block, so there is no stable structure for recommended feature ordering across one milestone.
tasks.solution_option: define one canonical top-level block for milestone-scoped feature execution order, including section headings, row format, numbering, and empty-section behavior.
concrete_steps: 1. Define the exact top-level heading and per-milestone subsection format to be inserted near the top of dev/FEATURE_PLANS.md.
2. Define the ordered row shape for one feature entry, including feature ID, feature title, and numbering semantics that remain parseable.
3. Decide and document whether empty milestone sections are removed or preserved after cleanup.

tasks.title: Add parser and lint support for milestone execution-order structure
tasks.problem: adding a new top-level block to FEATURE_PLANS can break plan parsing or linting unless the new structure is recognized and validated explicitly.
tasks.solution_option: extend parsing and lint logic so the milestone execution-order block is accepted when valid and rejected deterministically when malformed.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit the current FEATURE_PLANS parsing and lint paths in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py, especially section discovery, issue-order parsing, and plan-lint validation.
2. Add structure-aware parsing and validation so the new milestone execution-order block can coexist with feature sections without breaking `_find_h2_section_bounds` or plan-lint output.
3. Keep malformed-block failures deterministic so planning errors remain actionable.

tasks.title: Update planning schema and delta contracts for execution-order metadata
tasks.problem: if planning or delta payloads need to carry execution-order metadata, the repository needs one explicit contract instead of ad hoc inline handling.
tasks.solution_option: update relevant planning schema and delta contracts so milestone execution-order metadata can be represented explicitly where needed.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit whether planning schema or delta payloads such as dev/map/ISSUE_CREATE_INPUT_SCHEMA.md, sync_delta inputs, or tracker JSON contracts need explicit execution-order metadata.
2. Extend only the relevant schema or delta contracts where milestone execution-order data must be represented explicitly.
3. Keep the new metadata contract minimal so execution-order fields do not leak into unrelated planning surfaces.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for execution-order block structure handling
tasks.problem: without direct regression coverage, the new global execution-order block could silently break linting or drift into malformed states during later planning changes.
tasks.solution_option: add tests that lock valid structure handling and malformed-block rejection for the milestone execution-order block.
concrete_steps: 1. Add tests for valid milestone execution-order block parsing and plan-lint behavior in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py.
2. Add malformed-structure tests that verify deterministic lint or parser failures when the global block is broken.
3. Verify ordinary feature plan sections remain valid when the new global block is present above them.

tasks.title: Define planning ownership and insertion rules for feature execution order
tasks.problem: milestone execution order should be planning-owned rather than registration-owned, but the repository needs one explicit rule for when features enter or move within the order block.
tasks.solution_option: define plan feature as the owner of execution-order entries and specify append, reorder, and replan behavior for milestone-scoped feature ordering.
concrete_steps: 1. Define in planning docs and runtime expectations when `plan feature` inserts a feature into the milestone execution-order block and when existing order must be preserved.
2. Decide whether the default behavior is append-only, dependency-aware insertion, or explicit user-authored ordering and tie that decision to one code path.
3. Keep unplanned-but-created features out of the execution-order block by contract and by runtime behavior.

tasks.title: Integrate execution-order maintenance into plan feature flows
tasks.problem: once the new block exists, plan feature needs one deterministic path that creates or updates execution-order entries instead of leaving the block manual and unsynchronized.
tasks.solution_option: update plan feature flows so planned features are inserted or updated in the milestone execution-order block automatically and predictably.
concrete_steps: 1. Update plan initialization or plan write flows in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so the milestone execution-order block is created or updated when one feature plan is authored.
2. Preserve deterministic behavior across repeat planning runs so execution-order entries are updated or preserved instead of duplicated.
3. Keep feature title changes and replan scenarios aligned with the chosen ownership rules.

tasks.title: Integrate execution-order maintenance into planning sync or delta flows
tasks.problem: planning sync and reconciliation paths can drift from authored milestone execution order if they do not understand the new global block and its metadata contract.
tasks.solution_option: extend sync or delta-driven planning flows so milestone execution-order maintenance stays consistent across authored and synchronized planning operations.
concrete_steps: 1. Audit plan-sync, plan-task reconciliation, and delta-driven planning paths in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py and related contracts for execution-order awareness.
2. Update the relevant sync or delta flow so milestone execution-order entries remain aligned with feature planning state after automated writes.
3. Avoid duplicating order-maintenance logic between authored plan flows and sync paths by reusing one helper.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for append, replan, and title-update cases
tasks.problem: execution-order maintenance can drift quickly under repeat planning or title changes if append and update behavior is not covered directly.
tasks.solution_option: add regression coverage that locks the chosen insertion rules and repeat-planning behavior for milestone execution order.
concrete_steps: 1. Add tests for the first planned feature in a milestone and later appended features in the new execution-order block.
2. Add tests for repeat planning of an existing feature so ordering stays stable unless explicitly changed by the chosen rule set.
3. Add title-change coverage so displayed execution-order rows stay accurate without duplicating entries.

tasks.title: Define confirm cleanup contract for milestone execution order
tasks.problem: once planning owns milestone execution-order entries, confirm feature needs one explicit rule for when and how those entries are removed during feature completion.
tasks.solution_option: define confirm feature cleanup semantics for milestone execution-order rows, including dry-run versus write reporting and empty-section behavior.
concrete_steps: 1. Define inside confirm semantics when `_handle_confirm_feature_done` removes one feature row from the milestone execution-order block and what preview fields are returned in dry-run mode.
2. Define how empty milestone sections are handled after the last planned feature is removed from that block.
3. Keep execution-order cleanup aligned with the rest of the FEATURE_PLANS cleanup semantics.

tasks.title: Implement milestone execution-order cleanup during confirm feature
tasks.problem: confirm feature currently has no runtime awareness of milestone execution-order entries, so completed features would remain in the recommended queue after confirmation.
tasks.solution_option: extend confirm feature cleanup so milestone execution-order entries are removed in the same write run as other planning and tracker cleanup artifacts.
concrete_steps: 1. Add milestone execution-order cleanup to `_handle_confirm_feature_done` using the finalized block structure and one helper in dev/workflow_lib/confirm_commands.py.
2. Remove the feature row in the same write run as other FEATURE_PLANS cleanup so confirm does not leave partial planning state behind.
3. Keep empty-section handling and cleanup payload reporting deterministic.

tasks.title: Update workflows and docs for milestone execution-order lifecycle
tasks.problem: users will not understand when execution-order entries are created, maintained, and removed unless workflows and planning docs describe the new lifecycle explicitly.
tasks.solution_option: update planning and confirm docs so they explain milestone execution-order ownership, maintenance, and cleanup across the lifecycle.
concrete_steps: 1. Update plan-feature and related planning docs to explain when a planned feature enters the milestone execution-order block.
2. Update confirm workflow guidance to explain when completed features are removed from that block during feature confirmation.
3. Keep milestone planning semantics and execution-order wording aligned across workflow and protocol docs.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for confirm cleanup and empty-section handling
tasks.problem: milestone execution-order cleanup can regress on dry-run previews, write-mode deletion, or empty-section behavior unless the confirm flow is tested directly.
tasks.solution_option: add regression coverage for confirm feature cleanup of execution-order entries, including empty milestone-section handling and deterministic payloads.
concrete_steps: 1. Add dry-run tests for execution-order cleanup preview fields returned from `_handle_confirm_feature_done`.
2. Add write-mode tests for successful feature-row removal from the milestone execution-order block in FEATURE_PLANS.
3. Add empty-section tests so cleanup remains predictable when the last feature in a milestone is confirmed done.

tasks.title: Add canonical CLI surface for get context feature|issue
tasks.problem: Execution context retrieval is not exposed as canonical CLI commands; agents must infer context by manually scanning multiple trackers.
tasks.solution_option: Add one explicit command surface for workflow get context feature|issue with deterministic argument parsing and output contract discovery via help.
concrete_steps: Update dev/workflow_lib/cli.py command tree to register a get context router and keep command grammar deterministic for both feature and issue targets.
Add parser registration and argument validation wiring in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py (or extracted command module) for feature <id> and issue <id> selectors.
Ensure CLI help output and parser errors for missing/invalid IDs are deterministic and machine-readable for scripted workflow usage.
Validate with targeted CLI help tests under tests/workflow/test_core.py for the new command surface.

tasks.title: Implement feature-scoped context collector payload
tasks.problem: Feature execution currently depends on ad hoc manual reads of FEATURE_PLANS, TASK_LIST, TASK_EXECUTION_PIPELINE, and DEV_MAP, which is error-prone.
tasks.solution_option: Implement one feature-scoped collector that resolves ownership, task joins, and overlap intersections in one deterministic payload.
concrete_steps: Implement handler logic in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py to resolve <feature_id> in DEV_MAP and gather feature issue/task ownership.
Reuse FEATURE_PLANS section extractors to return the full ## <feature_id> block as raw markdown payload field.
Load TASK_LIST.json via tracker_store helpers, join mapped task IDs to full task objects, and return unresolved-reference errors deterministically.
Load TASK_EXECUTION_PIPELINE.json, compute overlap rows where overlap task IDs intersect feature task set, and include matched IDs plus full overlap rows in response.
Add focused tests in tests/workflow/test_feature_lifecycle.py for payload fields: plan block, mapped tasks, and overlap intersections.

tasks.title: Implement issue-scoped context collector payload
tasks.problem: Issue-chain execution has no dedicated collector; it lacks a canonical way to fetch only issue-relevant plan/task/overlap context.
tasks.solution_option: Implement issue-scoped collector path that resolves owner feature, extracts exact issue-plan block, and returns issue-task overlap intersections.
concrete_steps: Add issue resolver path in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py to locate issue owner feature and validate uniqueness in DEV_MAP.
Extract exact issue plan block (### <issue_id> - ...) from owning feature section in dev/FEATURE_PLANS.md with deterministic missing-block errors.
Join issue task IDs to task-list objects from TASK_LIST.json and compute issue-scoped overlaps from TASK_EXECUTION_PIPELINE.json intersections.
Return compact JSON payload aligned with feature-scoped contract while preserving issue-specific fields (issue_id, owner feature_id).
Add tests for success and invalid issue ID failure paths in tests/workflow/test_feature_lifecycle.py.

tasks.title: Update execute-feature and execute-issue workflows to require context commands
tasks.problem: Execution workflows still describe manual tracker reads, which can drift from the new canonical context command contract.
tasks.solution_option: Rewrite execution workflow steps to require workflow get context feature|issue before ordering and gate checks.
concrete_steps: Update .agents/workflows/execute-feature.md initialization/read steps to call workflow get context feature <feature_id> first.
Update .agents/workflows/execute-issue.md initialization/read steps to call workflow get context issue <issue_id> first.
Remove or reword manual multi-file read instructions that duplicate collector responsibilities.
Add explicit workflow examples using canonical command names and selectors only.

tasks.title: Align execution protocol wording with context-command read contract
tasks.problem: Protocol read-order text does not explicitly define context-command payload completeness checks for execution flows.
tasks.solution_option: Update protocol text so execution validates collector payload completeness before task execution begins.
concrete_steps: Update .agents/protocols/task-execution-protocol.md read-order and standards wording to reference context-command usage for feature/issue chain preparation.
Add explicit completeness criteria in protocol text: non-empty relevant plan block, resolved mapped tasks in scope, and overlap payload field present.
Keep existing materialization and confirmation gates unchanged while clarifying that context collection is a mandatory pre-execution contract.
Cross-check protocol wording against .agents/workflows/execute-feature.md and .agents/workflows/execute-issue.md to avoid duplicated or conflicting instructions.

tasks.title: Add CLI regression coverage for get context command surface
tasks.problem: Without regression tests, command-surface changes can silently break parser grammar or hide new context commands from help output.
tasks.solution_option: Extend workflow CLI tests to assert discoverability and stable syntax for workflow get context feature|issue.
concrete_steps: Extend tests/workflow/test_core.py to assert top-level help includes get and nested help includes context, feature, and issue selectors.
