Add parser error coverage for missing required --id or invalid ID format as deterministic failures.
Ensure existing help assertions remain valid after command-tree updates and remove stale expectations tied to legacy naming.

tasks.title: Add payload and negative-path tests for feature and issue context commands
tasks.problem: Context collectors need contract-level tests to prevent payload drift and to guarantee deterministic diagnostics for broken tracker states.
tasks.solution_option: Add fixture-based tests for successful payload extraction and for invalid/missing context conditions.
concrete_steps: Add feature-context tests in tests/workflow/test_feature_lifecycle.py for returned plan block, mapped task list entries, and overlap intersections.
Add issue-context tests validating owner resolution and exact issue-block extraction from feature plan sections.
Add negative tests for unknown feature/issue IDs and unresolved task references between DEV_MAP and TASK_LIST.
Assert error messages and exit codes are deterministic for CI and scripted agent use.

tasks.title: Add naming-regression checks for execute workflow command strings
tasks.problem: Execution docs can regress to stale command names, causing agents to run obsolete context lookup flows.
tasks.solution_option: Add checks that enforce canonical command strings in execution workflows and block reintroduction of legacy wording.
concrete_steps: Add regression assertions in workflow tests/smoke checks that .agents/workflows/execute-feature.md references workflow get context feature.
Add regression assertions that .agents/workflows/execute-issue.md references workflow get context issue.
Add negative check patterns to fail CI when legacy context-command wording appears in execution workflow docs.

tasks.title: Rewrite overlap workflow around full editable snapshots
tasks.problem: The current overlap workflow text still mixes scoped discovery with ambiguous delta-apply semantics, so agents have no single explicit contract for how full ISSUE_OVERLAPS drafts should be prepared and applied.
tasks.solution_option: Rewrite the overlap workflow around one explicit model: discovery remains scoped, the agent prepares a full editable ISSUE_OVERLAPS snapshot, and CLI is reserved for validation and final apply.
concrete_steps: Rewrite .agents/workflows/build-overlaps.md so the procedure explicitly starts with scope discovery commands, then has the agent read the current dev/ISSUE_OVERLAPS.json block and prepare one full editable draft snapshot.

State in the workflow that the draft contains both overlaps and issue_execution_order, and that the order field is edited explicitly by the agent instead of being treated as an implicit CLI side effect.
Remove or rewrite steps that imply partial-delta apply semantics, hidden merge behavior, or automatic recomputation during apply.
Cross-check workflow wording against the actual overlap commands in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so the documented sequence matches the implemented command surface.

tasks.title: Align overlap command descriptions with snapshot contract
tasks.problem: Overlap command help text and emitted semantics still describe delta-oriented behavior, which conflicts with the new contract where the agent prepares a full draft and CLI only validates and writes it.
tasks.solution_option: Update overlap command descriptions and related output wording in the parser/handlers so build/show/apply semantics match the full-snapshot workflow contract.
concrete_steps: Update overlap parser help strings in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py for build-overlaps, show-overlaps, and apply-overlaps so they describe scoped discovery plus full-draft validation/apply semantics.

Adjust overlap command comments and any emitted action/summary wording in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py that still implies hidden merge, scope replacement, or automatic order generation during apply.
Make the command-surface wording consistent with the rewritten workflow doc so agents can rely on one stable explanation of CLI-owned versus agent-owned responsibilities.

tasks.title: Extend overlap schema and contract for full-draft apply
tasks.problem: The current overlap contract validates basic pair shape, but it does not clearly encode the final full-draft apply payload as the canonical ISSUE_OVERLAPS block with explicit order semantics.
tasks.solution_option: Update the schema and contract validators so apply-overlaps expects one full ISSUE_OVERLAPS payload with explicit issue_execution_order and deterministic pair constraints.
concrete_steps: Update dev/map/ISSUE_OVERLAPS_JSON_SCHEMA.json so the canonical payload shape matches the full draft the agent applies, including schema_version, issue_execution_order, and overlaps.
Extend validate_issue_overlaps_contract_payload in dev/workflow_lib/tracker_json_contracts.py to enforce the final full-draft structure and keep pair-key uniqueness checks deterministic.
Keep dependency overlap order validation aligned with canonical issue pair membership so only valid order strings for the declared pair are accepted.

tasks.title: Implement semantic validation for overlap draft apply
tasks.problem: Schema checks alone do not prevent apply-overlaps from accepting drafts that reference unknown issues, duplicate canonical pairs, or manual order blocks that contradict declared dependency edges.
tasks.solution_option: Add semantic validation in apply-overlaps so invalid full drafts fail before any file mutation happens.
concrete_steps: Add semantic validation in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so apply-overlaps rejects overlap drafts with issue IDs that do not exist in dev/map/DEV_MAP.json.
Reject duplicate canonical issue pairs and reject dependency order strings that do not reference the same two issue IDs as their overlap entry.
Validate the supplied issue_execution_order against dependency overlaps so contradictory or cyclic manual ordering fails deterministically before write.

tasks.title: Persist validated overlap drafts without hidden recomputation
tasks.problem: apply-overlaps currently rewrites overlap state with implicit replacement semantics and hidden issue_execution_order recomputation, so the stored file does not necessarily match the draft the agent reviewed.
tasks.solution_option: Change apply-overlaps to persist the validated full draft block exactly as supplied and stop mutating issue_execution_order behind the agent's back.
concrete_steps: Refactor _handle_plan_apply_overlaps in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so it loads the draft payload, validates it, and writes the resulting full ISSUE_OVERLAPS block without building a new order block internally.
Remove code paths that derive issue_execution_order from all active DEV_MAP issues during apply-overlaps and keep write behavior tied to the validated draft payload.
Preserve the canonical top-level ISSUE_OVERLAPS.json structure on write while making the final persisted file equal to the reviewed draft content.

tasks.title: Fix feature-scoped overlap reads for F17-style subtree queries
tasks.problem: Feature-scoped overlap reads currently behave like direct issue filtering, so show-overlaps can miss overlaps that belong to the selected feature subtree and the agent loses reliable context for draft preparation.
tasks.solution_option: Update feature-scoped overlap reads to resolve the selected feature's issue IDs and filter overlaps by membership in that subtree.
concrete_steps: Update feature-scoped overlap filtering in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so show-overlaps --feature-id collects the selected feature's issue IDs from DEV_MAP and matches overlaps against that subtree.
Keep issue-scoped filtering behavior intact while making feature-scope output deterministic for features like F17-M1 that have multiple internal overlap pairs.
Review related overlap read/build helper paths in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so they no longer imply issue-only filtering when invoked with a feature selector.

tasks.title: Document full-draft overlap preparation and apply flow
tasks.problem: Even with runtime fixes, agents will keep drifting if the workflow and examples do not clearly show how to prepare a full overlap draft, edit issue_execution_order, validate it, and apply it.
tasks.solution_option: Rewrite overlap guidance and examples so the documented procedure mirrors the agent-prepared full-draft model end to end.
concrete_steps: Update .agents/workflows/build-overlaps.md examples to show scoped discovery, reading the current dev/ISSUE_OVERLAPS.json state, assembling a full draft, editing issue_execution_order, and then running CLI validation/apply.
Make command examples use the actual overlap commands and payload fields implemented in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py instead of describing implicit delta merge behavior.
Keep the workflow wording explicit about the separation of responsibilities: the agent prepares the draft, and CLI validates plus writes the final block.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for snapshot apply and scope reads
tasks.problem: The overlap workflow has already drifted once, and without dedicated regression tests future changes can reintroduce destructive apply behavior or broken feature-scope reads.
tasks.solution_option: Add overlap command tests that lock feature-scope reads, full-draft apply behavior, and invalid draft rejection into the workflow test suite.
concrete_steps: Extend tests/workflow/test_overlap_commands.py with feature-scope read coverage so show-overlaps --feature-id returns overlaps for all issues inside the selected feature subtree.
Add apply-overlaps tests that use full ISSUE_OVERLAPS drafts, preserve unrelated existing pairs when the draft preserves them, and fail deterministically on malformed or semantically invalid drafts.
Cover duplicate pair rejection, unknown issue IDs, pair/order mismatch, and contradictory manual issue_execution_order cases in the overlap test suite.

tasks.title: Restrict overlap order nodes to dependency participants
tasks.problem: issue_execution_order is currently seeded from all active DEV_MAP issues, so the stored order block is noisy and includes issues that do not participate in any dependency overlap.
tasks.solution_option: Change the global overlap order builder to derive ordered_issue_ids only from issues that appear in dependency overlaps.
concrete_steps: Update _build_global_issue_execution_order in dev/workflow_lib/feature_commands.py so it seeds graph nodes from dependency overlap participants instead of from all active issues in DEV_MAP.
Constrain or replace _collect_active_issue_ids_in_dev_map_order usage for overlap ordering so non-participating issues stop appearing in ordered_issue_ids.
Keep stable ordering and cycle handling for dependency-linked issues while making overlap-free graphs produce an empty order block instead of a full active-issue list.

tasks.title: Add noisy-order regression tests for isolated issues
tasks.problem: Without targeted tests, future overlap changes can quietly reintroduce isolated active issues into ordered_issue_ids and hide the regression behind otherwise valid overlap data.
tasks.solution_option: Add regression tests proving that only dependency participants appear in issue_execution_order while dependency-linked issues remain in stable topological order.
concrete_steps: Extend tests/workflow/test_overlap_commands.py with fixtures where only a subset of active issues participates in dependency overlaps and assert that ordered_issue_ids excludes isolated issues.
Add coverage for empty or overlap-free dependency graphs so the stored order block remains empty instead of echoing unrelated DEV_MAP issues.
Assert that dependency-linked issues still appear in stable order after the node-selection logic is narrowed to overlap participants only.
